name: Challenge → Resolution
summary: Identify a current business challenge, trace its origins, and recommend an actionable resolution. Useful for management consulting, internal strategy memos, and any deck where the audience needs a clear path from problem to plan.

beats.name: Hook
beats.description: Open with a specific, visible signal of the challenge — a metric heading the wrong way, a customer story, a competitive event. The hook should make the audience nod that yes, this matters.

beats.name: Dilemma
beats.description: State the challenge plainly. What is failing, who is feeling it, and why has it now risen to a level that demands a decision? The dilemma must feel real, not academic.

beats.name: Backdrop
beats.description: Provide the context behind the dilemma: how it formed, what's been tried, what constraints exist. Just enough history that the audience trusts your diagnosis without drowning in detail.

beats.name: Remedy
beats.description: Propose the resolution. Lead with the recommendation, then sketch the approach. Be opinionated — vague remedies invite vague responses.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.description: Demonstrate why the remedy will work. Comparable cases, supporting data, or pilot results. Choose evidence that addresses the audience's most likely objection, not the one easiest to answer.

beats.name: Motivate
beats.description: Make the case for acting now. Quantify the cost of delay and the upside of decisive action. Help the audience picture the room a quarter from now if they say yes.

beats.name: Conclusion
beats.description: Recap the challenge, the remedy, and the specific decision you need from the audience. End with a clear ask, not a summary slide.
